mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in industrial production index INDPRO reflects expanding manufacturing activity, which directly boosts corporate earnings expectations and leads to positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: If INDPRO increases by more than 1% over three months but the S&P 500 60-day forward return is negative in more than 40% of such occurrences over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising nonfarm payrolls PAYEMS indicates robust labor market conditions that support consumer spending and corporate profits, driving positive S&P 500 returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: If PAYEMS rises month-over-month but the subsequent 20-day S&P 500 return is negative in more than half of the observations over a 3-year period.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate FEDFUNDS exceeds 5%, tight monetary policy restricts economic activity through higher borrowing costs, compressing equity valuations and leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the following 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If in periods when FEDFUNDS > 5%, the S&P 500 20-day forward return is positive in more than 60% of instances, or if the average return is not significantly negative at the 5% level over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts HOUST falling below 1400 thousand units signals weakening residential investment and consumer confidence, which historically precedes declines in equity prices over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: If when HOUST < 1400, the S&P 500 20-day forward return is positive in more than 50% of cases, or the average return is not negative with 95% confidence over a 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: The joint condition of high federal funds rate and low unemployment rate creates a 'policy squeeze': the Fed is forced to keep rates high in a tight labor market, increasing recession risk and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If the joint condition occurs but the S&P 500 20-day forward return is positive in more than 60% of triggered periods, or if the interaction term in a linear regression has a coefficient not significantly different from zero at the 5% level.

mechanism_narrative: When both retail sales RSAFS and industrial production INDPRO decline simultaneously, it signals broad-based economic weakness that is more damaging than either series alone, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over a quarterly horizon.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS month-over-month decline AND INDPRO month-over-month decline
failure_condition: If simultaneous declines in RSAFS and INDPRO are followed by positive S&P 500 60-day returns in more than 40% of cases, or if the interaction variable's predictive power is not statistically significant.

mechanism_narrative: In a tight labor market where UNRATE is below 4%, wage pressures compress corporate margins and the Fed maintains a hawkish stance, which pressures equity valuations over a one-week horizon.
failure_condition: If in periods of UNRATE < 4%, the S&P 500 5-day forward return is positive in more than 55% of cases over any 2-year rolling window, or if the average return is not negative.

mechanism_narrative: During a late-cycle expansion phase marked by elevated GDPC1 but slowing momentum, forward returns become negative as investors anticipate a downturn; the signal is strongest when GDPC1 growth is positive but less than 2% annualized.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 growth rate positive but < 2% annualized over previous quarter
failure_condition: If in quarters with GDPC1 growth between 0% and 2% annualized, the S&P 500 60-day forward return is positive in more than 50% of cases over any 5-year rolling window.
